title: rubus × propinquiformis
intro: Rubus × propinquiformis, also known as the black raspberry or Rubus fruticosus, is a hybrid plant species in the Rubus genus, native to North America and parts of Asia. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly anthocyanins and ellagic acid, which have been found to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Rubus × propinquiformis include its potential to protect against cardiovascular disease, cancer, and cognitive decline, as well as its ability to support immune function and reduce inflammation. Rubus × propinquiformis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or supplement, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
